title: Daily Briefing — May 19
headline: Bonds blinking yellow, sector tides turning, but tech holding the 20 MA line... for now.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us — SPY ($735.56) and QQQ ($704.69) both grinding just above their respective SMA20s, but HYG ($79.38) and TLT ($82.99) are both on approach/triggered bear signals. Per my first rule: if bonds freak out, everything breaks. Equity exposure here needs to be sized down while bonds test support.

Energy (XLE) stays the canary in the green zone (RS=103.8, leading, SMA20 at $58.04 and price at $60.54), but look at tech: XLK ($174.86) still in the leadership quadrant but 'weakening' on rotation, and Financials (XLF $51.44) stuck below its own 20-day. Defensive sectors (XLP, XLV, XLU, XLRE) are quietly improving, so the rotation is underway towards safety.

Fear & Greed is at 62.2 — not redline yet, but greed decaying. VIX is normal but sneaking higher (17.98 > SMA20). No outright panic, but with TLT below all MAs and HYG threatening, this feels like a late-cycle setup where consensus is still a little too comfortable. Patience, react, don’t predict — look for failed retests and wait for a clean sector rotation, not a hero move.
callout_body:
SPY: Watch $726.88 (SMA20) — daily close below = caution
HYG: $79.38 (nearing breakdown), TLT: Triggered below $85.30 (SMA20)
XLK: $167.54 (SMA20) support
XLF: $51.61 (SMA20) — needs to reclaim for bulls
XLE is the only sector firmly above all major MAs
AAII not provided; Fear & Greed not extreme, but watch for cracks
Don’t chase — abundance mindset!
action_items: Reduce equity exposure and stop adding to tech if HYG and TLT both close red
Look for rotation opportunities in Energy (XLE, OIH) and defensive sectors (XLP, XLV) — don’t front-run, wait for daily closes above respective SMA20s
closing_quote: When bonds blink, equities flinch — follow the flows, not the headlines.
